title: LCP Ruby Model Definition
description: Schema for model metadata that defines database structure, validations, associations, scopes, and events. Models are defined in config/lcp_ruby/models/ as YAML files or via Ruby DSL.

name.description: Unique model identifier. Used to reference the model from presenters, permissions, and associations.

label.description: Human-readable singular name for the model. Falls back to humanized name if omitted.

label_plural.description: Human-readable plural name for the model. Falls back to Rails pluralization of label.

table_name.description: Override the database table name. Defaults to Rails convention (pluralized snake_case). Use '_virtual' for metadata-only models (no AR class or DB table).

fields.description: Database columns and their configuration. Each field becomes an ActiveRecord attribute with optional validations and transforms.

associations.description: ActiveRecord associations (belongs_to, has_many, has_one) with other LCP models or external AR classes.

validations.description: Model-level validations not attached to a specific field (e.g., cross-field comparisons, custom validators).

scopes.description: Named query scopes for filtering records. Simple scopes use where/order/limit; complex scopes use type: custom or type: parameterized.

events.description: Lifecycle and field-change events dispatched to registered handlers in app/event_handlers/.

display_templates.description: Named templates for rendering model records in different contexts (e.g., association lists, search results). Keys are template names.

positioning.description: Enable drag-and-drop record ordering via the positioning gem. true uses defaults; object form allows customization.

field.description: Column name storing the position integer.

scope.description: Scope positioning within a parent (e.g., position per category). String for single scope, array for composite.

indexes.description: Database indexes to create on the model's table. SchemaManager creates these at boot alongside the table.

aggregates.description: Virtual aggregate columns computed from associated records via SQL subqueries or custom services. Each key is the aggregate name, which becomes a virtual attribute referenceable in presenters.

label_method.description: Method called on a record to produce its display label (used in association selects, display templates, etc.).

custom_fields.description: Enable runtime user-defined custom fields stored in a JSON column. Requires the custom_fields generator.

soft_delete.description: Enable soft delete (discard/restore) instead of permanent destruction. Records are marked with a timestamp column rather than deleted.

column.description: Name of the timestamp column for soft delete.

auditing.description: Enable change tracking. Records field-level diffs on create/update/destroy into an audit log model.

only.description: Whitelist: only track changes to these fields. Mutually exclusive with 'ignore'.

ignore.description: Blacklist: track all fields except these.

track_associations.description: Include association FK changes in audit diffs.

track_attachments.description: Include Active Storage attachment changes in audit diffs.

expand_custom_fields.description: Expand custom_fields JSON column into individual field diffs instead of logging raw JSON.

expand_json_fields.description: List of JSON column names to expand into individual key diffs.

userstamps.description: Automatically track which user created/updated each record via FK columns pointing to the user model.

created_by.description: FK column name for the creating user.

updated_by.description: FK column name for the last updating user.

store_name.description: Also store the user's display name in a separate column (e.g., created_by_name) as a snapshot for display without joins.

tree.description: Enable self-referential tree hierarchy with parent/children associations, traversal methods, and cycle detection.

parent_field.description: FK column pointing to the parent record. Must be declared in fields as integer type.

children_name.description: Name of the auto-generated has_many association for child records.

parent_name.description: Name of the auto-generated belongs_to association for the parent record.

dependent.description: What happens to children when a parent is destroyed.

max_depth.description: Maximum nesting depth for cycle detection and traversal limits.

ordered.description: Enable position-based ordering of siblings within the same parent. Requires a position field.

position_field.description: Column name for sibling ordering when ordered: true.

field.description: A database column with type, validations, and optional transforms.

name.description: Column name in the database. Must be unique within the model.

type.description: Data type. Base types: string, text, integer, float, decimal, boolean, date, datetime, enum, file, rich_text, json, uuid, attachment, array. Custom types (email, phone, url, color) and user-defined types from config/lcp_ruby/types/ are also valid.

item_type.description: Element type for array fields. Required when type is 'array'.

label.description: Human-readable label. Falls back to humanized field name.

default.description: Default value for new records. Type must match the field type.

transforms.description: Value transforms applied before save: strip, downcase, upcase, normalize_url, normalize_phone, etc.

validations.description: Field-level validations applied to this column.

enum_values.description: For enum fields: list of allowed values. Can be strings or {value, label} objects.

computed.description: Read-only computed field. String form is a Ruby expression; object form uses a service.

sequence.description: Auto-numbering sequence field. Assigns a unique sequential value on record creation. Requires the gapfree_sequences generator. Use `true` as shorthand for all defaults (global scope, raw counter, start 1, step 1).

scope.description: Scope columns or virtual keys (_year, _month, _day). Counter resets independently per unique scope combination.

format.description: Template for the final value. Supports %{sequence}, %{sequence:Nd} (zero-padded), %{_year}, %{_month}, %{_day}, and %{field_name}.

start.description: Initial counter value for a new scope.

readonly.description: When true, the field is rendered as a disabled input in forms (visible but not editable).

assign_on.description: When to assign: 'create' (before_create only) or 'always' (also fills blank values on update).

options.description: Type-specific options. For attachments: content_type, max_size, max_files, variants. For other types: custom options passed to the type handler.

source.description: Data source for virtual/external fields. 'external' means the field is not backed by a DB column. Object form uses a service (e.g., json_field extractor).

service.description: Data provider service name from app/lcp_services/data_providers/.

options.description: Options passed to the data provider service.

column_options.description: Database column options passed directly to the Rails migration column definition.

limit.description: Maximum length for string columns or byte size for binary columns.

null.description: Whether the column allows NULL values.

precision.description: Total number of digits for decimal columns.

scale.description: Number of digits after the decimal point for decimal columns.

validation.description: A validation rule applied to a field or at model level.

type.description: Validation type. Maps to Rails validators (e.g., presence → validates_presence_of).

options.description: Options passed to the validator (e.g., {minimum: 3} for length, {greater_than: 0} for numericality).

when.description: Conditional validation — only runs when the condition is met.

validator_class.description: Fully qualified class name for type: custom validators.

field_ref.description: Reference to another field for cross-field validations (e.g., comparison).

service.description: Service class name for type: service validations.

field.description: Target field name for model-level validations (when validation is not nested under a field).

association.description: An ActiveRecord association linking this model to another model.

name.description: Association name used in code and presenter references.

target_model.description: Name of the associated LCP model. Omit for non-LCP (external AR) classes.

class_name.description: Explicit AR class name. Use when the association name doesn't match the model (e.g., 'manager' → 'User').

foreign_key.description: FK column name. Defaults to Rails convention ({name}_id for belongs_to).

dependent.description: Behavior when the owning record is destroyed.

required.description: For belongs_to: make the association mandatory (adds presence validation on FK).

inverse_of.description: Name of the reciprocal association on the target model. Helps Rails avoid extra queries.

counter_cache.description: For belongs_to: maintain a counter column on the parent. true uses Rails convention ({plural}_count), string overrides the column name.

touch.description: For belongs_to: touch the parent's updated_at when this record changes. String form touches a specific column.

polymorphic.description: For belongs_to: enable polymorphic association (stores type + id).

as.description: For has_many/has_one: the polymorphic interface name on the target model.

through.description: For has_many :through — name of the join association on this model.

source.description: For has_many :through — source association name on the join model when it differs from the association name.

autosave.description: Automatically save associated records when the parent is saved.

validate.description: Run validations on associated records when saving.

nested_attributes.description: Enable accepts_nested_attributes_for on this association, allowing form-based editing of associated records.

allow_destroy.description: Allow removing associated records via _destroy param.

reject_if.description: Proc name or :all_blank to reject empty nested records.

limit.description: Maximum number of nested records allowed.

update_only.description: Only allow updating existing records, not creating new ones.

scope.description: A named query scope for filtering records.

name.description: Scope name. Used in predefined filters, permission scopes, and URL params.

type.description: 'custom' means implemented in the host app as a class method. 'parameterized' accepts typed parameters from the UI.

where.description: Simple where clause. Keys are column names, values are match values.

where_not.description: Negated where clause. Generates scope with where.not(...).

order.description: Sort order applied by this scope.

limit.description: Maximum number of records returned by this scope.

parameters.description: Parameter definitions for type: parameterized scopes. Each parameter is cast from request params and passed to the scope method.

scope_parameter.description: A typed parameter for a parameterized scope. Values are cast from string request params at runtime.

name.description: Parameter name (used as the key in request params).

type.description: Parameter data type. Determines how the raw string value is cast.

label.description: Human-readable label for the parameter in the filter UI.

required.description: If true, the scope is skipped when this parameter is missing.

default.description: Default value used when the parameter is not provided.

values.description: For type: enum — list of allowed values. Input not in this list is rejected.

model.description: For type: model_select — the LCP model name to populate the select dropdown.

min.description: For numeric types — minimum allowed value (clamped, not rejected).

max.description: For numeric types — maximum allowed value (clamped, not rejected).

index.description: A database index created on the model's table by SchemaManager.

columns.description: Column names included in the index. Order matters for composite indexes.

unique.description: Create a unique index (enforces uniqueness constraint at the DB level).

name.description: Custom index name. Auto-generated if omitted.

event.description: A lifecycle or field-change event dispatched to registered handlers in app/event_handlers/.

name.description: Event name. For lifecycle events: after_create, after_update, before_destroy, after_destroy. For field_change: a custom name.

type.description: Event type. 'lifecycle' fires on AR callbacks; 'field_change' fires when a specific field value changes.

field.description: For type: field_change — the field name to monitor for changes.

condition.description: Optional condition that must be met for the event to fire.

display_template.description: Template for rendering a model record in association lists, search results, or menu badges. Uses {field_name} interpolation.

subtitle.description: Secondary line template shown below the main template.

icon.description: Icon name displayed alongside the template.

badge.description: Field name whose value is rendered as a badge next to the template.

renderer.description: Custom renderer name for the entire display template.

partial.description: ERB partial path for fully custom rendering.

options.description: Options passed to the renderer or partial.

data_source.description: External data source configuration. When present, the model reads from an API instead of the database. Phase 1 is read-only (index + show).

base_url.description: Base URL for REST JSON API. Required when type is 'rest_json'.

resource.description: API resource path (pluralized). Defaults to model name pluralized.

provider.description: Host-provided class name implementing the data source contract. Required when type is 'host'.

id_field.description: Remote field name for the primary key. Mapped to :id locally.

auth.description: Authentication configuration for REST JSON APIs.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing the bearer token.

username_env.description: Environment variable name for basic auth username.

password_env.description: Environment variable name for basic auth password.

header_name.description: Custom header name for header auth type.

value_env.description: Environment variable name for header auth value.

pagination.description: Pagination configuration for REST JSON APIs.

cache.description: Caching configuration. Wraps the adapter with CachedWrapper when present.

ttl.description: Cache TTL for individual record lookups (seconds).

stale_on_error.description: Serve expired cached data when the API is unreachable.

field_mapping.description: Maps local field names to remote API field names. Keys are local names, values are remote names.

supported_operators.description: Filter operators supported by this API. Unsupported operators are silently dropped.

endpoints.description: Custom endpoint configuration for REST JSON APIs.

path.description: URL path for show endpoint. Use :id placeholder.

response_path.description: Dot-path to navigate into JSON response.

condition.description: Conditional trigger: field-based condition object (with field/operator/value keys), service reference, or a method name string.

aggregate.description: A virtual aggregate column definition. Exactly one of three types must be used: declarative (function+association), custom SQL expression, or service-based computation.

oneOf.description: Declarative aggregate: SQL aggregate function over a has_many association.

association.description: Name of a has_many association on the model.

source_field.description: Field on the target model to aggregate. Required for sum/min/max/avg; optional for count (defaults to *).

where.description: Hash equality conditions on the target model. Supports :current_user placeholder for per-user aggregates.

distinct.description: Use DISTINCT in the aggregate function (e.g., COUNT(DISTINCT ...) or SUM(DISTINCT ...)).

default.description: Default value via COALESCE wrap. COUNT always defaults to 0 even without this setting.

include_discarded.description: When false (default), excludes soft-deleted records from the aggregate. Only applies when the target model uses soft_delete.

type.description: Explicit result type override. Normally inferred from function and source field type.

oneOf.description: Custom SQL aggregate: raw SQL subquery returning a single value.

sql.description: Custom SQL subquery returning a single value. Use %{table} placeholder for the parent model's table name.
examples: SELECT SUM(r.score * r.weight) / NULLIF(SUM(r.weight), 0) FROM ratings r WHERE r.project_id = %{table}.id

type.description: Result type. Required for SQL aggregates.

oneOf.description: Service-based aggregate: computed by a Ruby service class.

service.description: Service key looked up in app/lcp_services/aggregates/. The service class must implement self.call(record, options:).

type.description: Result type. Required for service aggregates.

options.description: Options hash passed to the service.
